task_name: NETWORK Runtime Integration Update — AWAKEN Runtime Events + Media Player v2
implementation_mode: Targeted integration on the existing near-finished system. Do not rewrite the shell, window manager, Supabase repositories, Atlas, MIND, Memory Card, admin, virtual filesystem, or boot flow.

existing_systems: BIOS, startup and guest login flow
Supabase content, community and filesystem repositories
event bus, runtime state and scheduling domain
project_context.principle: Wire the existing systems together and deepen the XP interaction model while preserving the approved AWAKEN identity.

inherit_previous_stabilization_work: clean post-boot desktop with no automatically opened app windows
working minimize, restore, focus, maximize and close behavior
Discord to Supabase to MIND initial fetch, Realtime and reconciliation
Archive opening and intentional empty states
Community opening MIND as a separate app above it
persistent selected wallpaper without changing the current AWAKEN Default
configurable remote icon overrides with bundled fallbacks
accurate clock and honest progress indicators

awaken_runtime_events.purpose: Promote the existing popup ad and intrusion work from an isolated demo into a controlled, stateful content-delivery system inside the real OS runtime.
awaken_runtime_events.current_problem: ads.js already contains security warnings, messenger toasts, dial-up, installer and Memory Card events, but it is not loaded by the main shell and its actions only replace popup copy.
requirements: Load the existing ad definitions through the main shell using the repository's current module conventions. Do not duplicate them into script.js.
Create one runtime-event controller that owns eligibility, scheduling, session limits, cooldowns, weights, active dates and global disable state.
Use src/system/event-bus.js, runtime-state.js and src/domain/scheduling.js where those abstractions fit.
Do not spawn any popup during BIOS, startup, login or immediately after the desktop first appears.
Automatic events require a deliberate user interaction followed by a configurable quiet period.
Allow complete disabling in Settings and through authorized admin configuration.
Limit concurrent windows, persist dismissals and frequency state locally, and respect reduced motion and sound settings.
Every event must be dismissible and must never trap focus or block the entire website.
Keep AWAKEN branding visible so these are clearly fictional in-world system events rather than deceptive real antivirus, browser or credential prompts.
Add a rare intrusion sequence only through an explicit terminal command, admin preview or tightly limited approved campaign.
The intrusion sequence must be cancellable and restore all filters, windows, focus and audio state when it ends.

memory_card: RECOVER must create real Memory Card entries through src/domain/memory-card.js or its current public service.
Use canonical fragment/content references rather than a fake counter.
Dispatch a shared domain event so the Memory Card UI updates immediately.
mind: Messenger events may use a recent eligible public MIND record.
Never expose private Discord channels or unapproved member data.
Fall back to approved static AWAKEN copy when no eligible MIND record exists.
OPEN MESSAGE should open MIND or the corresponding public record.
terminal: scan triggers a safe themed scan and optionally one eligible runtime event.
recover invokes the same Memory Card recovery service as the popup.
Commands must call shared services instead of duplicating effects.
admin: Add a Runtime Events section to the existing admin interface.
Authorized editors may enable events and edit weight, cooldown, maximum impressions and active dates.
Reuse managed content and scheduling repositories.
Anonymous users remain read-only and cannot alter event configuration.
Keep a local preview path while production publishing remains locked.
boot_handoff: BIOS/startup/login may write a small runtime handoff object.
Any boot message claiming a recovered fragment must correspond to a real seeded or queued Memory Card entry.
Boot must not depend on Supabase availability.

xp_authenticity_pass: Audit active and inactive titlebar gradients, inner highlights, bevels and text shadow rather than replacing working chrome blindly.
Taskbar buttons should use icon plus ellipsized title and an inset pressed state for the active window.
Support titlebar double-click maximize and restore.
Strengthen desktop icon label outline or shadow across every wallpaper.
Route startup, notify, error and taskbar sounds through one sound service with mute controls.
Complete desktop, icon and window context menus using only actions that genuinely work.
Do not replace the approved AWAKEN eye Start icon with a Windows flag.
acceptance_tests: ads.js is loaded by the real runtime without duplicate definitions
no popup appears during boot or initial desktop arrival
limits and cooldowns survive refresh
RECOVER writes actual Memory Card records exactly once
MIND-derived events only use approved public data
scan and recover use shared services
admin preview can enable, disable and test an event
the intrusion preview fully restores the desktop
events are keyboard accessible and dismissible

awaken_media_player_v2.prototype_reference: Use the supplied standalone AWAKEN XP Media Player HTML as a behavior and layout reference, not as an iframe final implementation.
awaken_media_player_v2.purpose: Turn the standalone prototype into a real managed desktop application connected to Atlas, approved media records, the A:\ filesystem and official platform destinations.
prototype_features_to_preserve: spectrum, oscilloscope and bars with peaks
playlist, previous, play, pause, stop and next
Apple Music, SoundCloud and Spotify embeds
requirements: Integrate the player into the current application registry, window manager, taskbar, icons and shared styles.
Do not iframe the standalone demo as the final application.
Keep local files in the browser; never upload them automatically.
Read public release data from Atlas/content/media repositories where available instead of creating conflicting hardcoded catalogs.
Only approved, public and verified records may appear in the release library.
Centralize Apple Music, SoundCloud, Spotify and YouTube URLs.
Add the official AWAKEN YouTube destination and approved video or playlist opening.
Replace dead Signal and Bars controls with real destinations or remove them.
Explain clearly that protected provider embeds cannot feed the custom EQ or analyser.
Allow AWAKEN-hosted or Supabase Storage audio to use the analyser only when publication state and CORS permit.
Open compatible files from the virtual A:\ filesystem through Explorer and terminal open commands.
Persist safe preferences such as volume, EQ enabled state, preset and visualization mode locally.
Do not autoplay provider streams when the app opens.
Avoid duplicate AudioContext instances when minimizing and restoring.
Closing must stop playback, cancel animation frames and release local object URLs.
Render filenames, URLs and external metadata without unsafe innerHTML.
Validate provider URLs before embedding or opening them.
Provide keyboard controls, visible focus and accessible labels.
Keep mobile controls usable without page overflow.
data_guidance: Reuse Atlas entities, source links, media links and contributor credits.
A playable source should contain provider, canonical URL, safe embed URL where supported, media type, publication state, duration and artwork.
Local tracks remain outside Supabase unless a later explicit upload workflow is approved.
acceptance_tests: the player opens, focuses, minimizes and restores as a normal managed window
local MP3, WAV and OGG playback works without upload
EQ, visualizer, seek and playlist work
verified releases render from the canonical repository
Apple Music, SoundCloud and Spotify URL validation works
YouTube destination works
unsupported links fail cleanly
Explorer opens compatible media files in the player
closing releases audio resources and object URLs
user-controlled metadata is rendered safely
mobile layout has no clipped controls or document overflow

implementation_order: audit the current main branch after the Atlas merge
run and preserve the existing test baseline
integrate Media Player v2 with the current repositories and window manager
create the shared runtime-event controller and load existing ads.js definitions
apply the scoped XP authenticity pass
run desktop, mobile, accessibility, security and regression verification
required_documentation: document event limits, scheduling, persistence and admin controls
required_verification: security review for unsafe HTML, external URLs, Supabase RLS and secrets
confirm no automatic popup appears during boot or initial desktop arrival
final_report: Memory Card, MIND, terminal and admin connections
Media Player integration and provider configuration
